item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just a Hanged King Tragedy reskin… until you find out the bands didn't actually die. Then it starts to get interesting.

But that new angle never actually goes anywhere. Like AlanDaris said, the incident log doesn't give us anything new, it just reinforces the "ooo, spooky" by adding children. I wanna know more about these bands that aren't actually the band. What are these people really eating? Is it something even worse than human meat? Explore the concept a little bit more in the article, and I'll be happy to give it an upvote. For now, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote for now, seems way too similar to 701, and other "event ritual" based scips, but with far less dire stakes, since the band is ok, and adults can be amnescticized.
It is an interesting addition at the end that there is a problem amnescticizing children in your canon, but I don't think this is a particularly good vendor of that idea, and the delivery doesn't quite pack the punch for a hook, since you already have children being cannibals has a hook, nor does it seem to set up a strong idea of related content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's 50/50. It's fine without, and part of my ongoing mission to use less commas, heh.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that I've noticed this, and will be addressing them for this behavior presently.

General note, in the future, feel free to reach out directly to staff if you notice problematic reviews being given.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So how do we know the dead ones aren't the real band members and the ones that call in sick aren't the imposters?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When the SCP-4244 event begins the crowd will quickly become violent…

Correct me if I'm wrong, but it sounds like there should be a comma here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one didn't work for me, unfortunately. My main problem here lies in the addendum: as I said in the chat, I feel like the trope of using children to enhance the horrifying and/or gross imagery is a little overdone and in this particular case it doesn't grab me. Although I liked how it said that the event "proceeded as normal", lol.

In my opinion, the incident doesn't function as a good ending for this article. The first half was good and bizarre, and in the last addendum I expected something that will either reveal some parts of this anomaly or will just leave me with a question "what even was that"? But the incident just fell flat and didn't deliver that punch I was hoping for. So I guess it wasn't to my taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral for me, sorry. It just feels like "mysterious thing what kills people." An interview log or incident report with a little more detail would make it for me, but I understand the beauty of simplicity and the subjectivity of taste.

E: Just reread it and I think the hook here is the crowd panic post-event (since the band is fine), I can imagine how freaky that would be and it's interesting me more thinking about it. Maybe a little more detail about that, or an interview with someone who was in the crowd at a later show, or with a parent or kid at the first show?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They've been leaving replies like that on a shit ton of articles, looking after your article there's like one mistake so I don't know where they're getting "Additionally, there are instances of improper grammar.".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently they missed the part in the post directly above theirs where I credited all the people who offered feedback on it and then linked to my author page with 20+ successful articles on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For example, the band that enters from the beginning of the SCP-4244 events. Are they exact copies or are they random people?

That's the entire point. We aren't supposed to know who/what is impersonating these bands, and more importantly why they are doing it. That's the main hook of this.

And what do you mean by "repost this to the Sandbox"? They've gotten a bunch of critique on this from the looks of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was looking for a bit more when reading this document. Moreover, there were a lot of plotholes that weren't resolved. For example, the band that enters from the beginning of the SCP-4244 events. Are they exact copies or are they random people? Additionally, there are instances of improper grammar. Please repost this to the Sandbox. Don't hesitate to contact me if you need anything at all.
Yours,
Dr. P.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This one is short and stupid, and the idea came to me while playing Civilisation 6 after my Rock Band unit MYSTERIOUSLY DISAPPEARED after a bad concert.

Thanks to Presque, AlanDaris, CaptainKirby and McB for reading the draft, and CaptainKirby for coming up with the title.

Find more of my stuff here!
